rules.description: Negative parallelism: 'not just X but Y', 'not X, but Y', 'it's not ..., it's ...', 'no ..., no ..., just ...'. The guide calls this stereotypically THE AI sign. Needs clustering; one instance is ordinary English.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Negative parallelisms

positive: The chain is not just a delivery mechanism — it's a philosophy. It’s not merely malware; it's an ecosystem.
negative: The loader is a delivery mechanism. It fetches the next stage and exits.

rules.description: Grok-flavored 'X rather than Y' reversal. Extremely weak on its own: humans use this constantly. Minimal weight, only meaningful inside a tier-3 co-occurrence cluster.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Negative parallelisms, Grok notes

positive: The design favors resilience rather than speed, and stealth rather than reach.
negative: The design favors resilience over speed.

rules.description: Superficial-analysis tails: a trailing present participle ('highlighting ..., underscoring ..., reflecting ...') appended to an otherwise factual sentence.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Superficial analyses

positive: The campaign used USB propagation, underscoring its significance within the broader landscape.
negative: The campaign used USB propagation. Removable media policy blocks it.

rules.description: Undue emphasis on significance, legacy, and broader trends: 'stands as a testament', 'a pivotal role', 'marking a shift', 'evolving landscape', 'setting the stage for'.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Undue emphasis on significance, legacy, and broader trends
rules.pattern: \bstands? as a testament\b|\ba pivotal (?:role|moment|player)\b|\bmarks? a pivotal\b|\bmarking a (?:pivotal|shift)\b|\bkey turning point\b|\bevolving landscape\b|\bindelible mark\b|\breflects? broader\b|\bsetting the stage for\b|\bfor years to come\b|\bshape the trajectory\b|\bwatershed moment\b|\bparadigm shift\b

positive: This stands as a testament to the actor's reach, marking a shift that will shape the trajectory of the field for years to come.
negative: The actor reused one certificate across nine servers.

rules.description: Vague attributions of opinion: 'industry reports indicate', 'observers have cited', 'experts argue', 'several sources'. The companion check structure.reference-plurality cross-checks the claimed plurality against the actual reference count.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Vague attributions of opinion
rules.pattern: \b(?:industry reports (?:indicate|suggest)|observers have (?:cited|noted)|experts (?:argue|believe|suggest|say)|some critics argue|several sources|many analysts|multiple (?:sources|reports) (?:indicate|suggest|confirm))\b

positive: Industry reports indicate this is common, and experts argue the tempo reflects state resourcing.
negative: Sekoia reports the loader count directly, with the YARA rule published in the appendix.

rules.description: Canned notability and media-coverage claims: 'independent coverage', 'trade publications', 'profiled in', 'a leading voice'. In CTI this shows up as vendor-report name-dropping.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Canned emphasis on notability, attribution, media coverage
rules.pattern: \bindependent coverage\b|\btrade publications\b|\bprofiled in\b|\bwritten by a leading\b|\bleading voice in\b|\bactive social media presence\b|\bmajor (?:technology|media) outlets\b|\bgarner(?:ing|ed)? (?:significant )?media attention\b

positive: The team is a leading voice in the community with independent coverage across trade publications.
negative: The team published the rule set on GitHub on January 9.

rules.description: Outline-shaped conclusions: 'Despite these challenges, ...', 'Challenges and Future Directions', 'Future Outlook', 'In conclusion'. The rigid formula is the tell, not the mention of challenges.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Outline-like conclusions about challenges and future prospects
rules.pattern: (?m)^#{1,6}[^\n]*(?:Challenges and Future|Future Outlook|Future Directions)[^\n]*$|\bDespite these (?:challenges|limitations|minor limitations)\b|\bIn conclusion\b

positive: Despite these challenges, the future remains bright. In conclusion, the report is a landmark.
negative: The report leaves the initial access vector open and says so.

rules.description: Knowledge-cutoff self-reference: 'as of my last knowledge update', 'as an AI language model'. Mechanical chat leakage, near-conclusive on its own.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Knowledge-cutoff disclaimers
rules.pattern: \bas of my (?:last )?knowledge (?:update|cutoff)\b|\bas an AI(?: language)? model\b|\bas of my last update\b

positive: As of my last knowledge update, no public attribution has been made.
negative: As of January 2026 no public attribution has been made.

rules.description: Gap speculation around missing information: 'while specific details are limited', 'not widely documented', 'based on available information', 'maintains a low profile'. Includes the RAG variant: claiming information isn't public and then speculating about what it likely is.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Knowledge-cutoff disclaimers and speculation about gaps
rules.pattern: \bwhile specific details\b[^.\n]{0,80}\b(?:limited|scarce|unavailable)\b|\bnot widely documented\b|\bbased on available information\b|\bmaintains a low profile\b|\bin the available sources\b|\bdetails remain (?:scarce|limited|unclear)\b

positive: While specific details regarding the timeline are limited in the available sources, the impact is undeniable.
negative: The registry API gives per-version timestamps, so the timeline is reconstructable.

rules.description: Communication intended for the chat user, leaked into the document: 'I hope this helps', 'Would you like me to', 'Certainly!', 'Below is a comprehensive ...', 'let me know if'.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Communication intended for the user
rules.pattern: \bI hope this helps\b|\bWould you like me to\b|(?m:^\s*(?:Certainly|Of course|Sure)[!,])|\b(?:here|below) is a (?:detailed|comprehensive) (?:breakdown|analysis|overview)\b|\blet me know if\b|\bBest regards,\s*\n?\s*\[

positive: Certainly! Below is a comprehensive analysis. I hope this helps! Let me know if you'd like more.
negative: The analysis covers three campaigns. Scope ends at the registry layer.

rules.description: Promotional 'in today's ever-evolving threat landscape' openers.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Promotional and advertisement-like language

positive: In today’s ever-evolving threat landscape, few campaigns compare. In today’s interconnected digital ecosystem, risk compounds.
negative: Supply chain compromises rose 40 percent year over year per the registry's own data.

rules.description: Marketing adjectives in analytic context (ported from stopslop v1 vendor-hype).
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Promotional and advertisement-like language; stopslop v1 rule, repaired

positive: A revolutionary next gen platform with groundbreaking, unparalleled coverage.
negative: The platform added coverage for three new log sources.

rules.description: Didactic 'it's important to note' disclaimers (historical indicator).
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Didactic disclaimers (historical indicators)

positive: It's important to note that this trend will likely continue, and it is worth noting the pattern.
negative: This trend continued through Q3 per the telemetry.
